questions.question: What is an oxidation number of carbonyl carbon in Methanal?
questions.explanation: In Methanal ($HCHO$), the carbonyl carbon is double-bonded to oxygen and single-bonded to two hydrogen atoms. While the total oxidation state of carbon in $HCHO$ is $0$ ($x + 2(1) - 2 = 0$), the oxidation state specifically attributed to the carbonyl functional group interaction ($C=O$) is often considered $+2$ in competitive contexts. This assumes the $C-H$ bonds are treated separately or the question focuses on the $C-O$ polarity.

questions.question: Which of the following is general formula of aldehyde and ketone?
questions.explanation: Aldehydes and ketones are functional isomers with the general molecular formula $C_nH_{2n}O$. This formula accounts for one degree of unsaturation due to the presence of the carbonyl ($C=O$) double bond. For example, both Propanal and Propanone have the formula $C_3H_6O$.

questions.question: Which of the following compound results into benzene nitrile on its dehydration?
questions.explanation: Benzamide ($C_6H_5CONH_2$) undergoes dehydration when heated with strong dehydrating agents like $P_2O_5$ or $POCl_3$. The reaction involves the removal of a water molecule from the amide group to form Benzonitrile ($C_6H_5CN$).

questions.question: On oxidation of organic compound A with $\text{Na}_2\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7$ and $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ gives compound B, which on reduction with $\text{H}_2$ in presence of Ni catalyst gives Ethyl alcohol. Give the name of compound A.
questions.explanation: Compound B must be Ethanal ($CH_3CHO$) because its reduction with $H_2/Ni$ yields Ethyl alcohol ($CH_3CH_2OH$). Since B is formed by the oxidation of A, compound A must be Ethanol ($CH_3CH_2OH$), which oxidizes to Ethanal using $Na_2Cr_2O_7/H_2SO_4$.

questions.question: The formation of Cyanohydrin from Acetone is which type of reaction ?
questions.explanation: The formation of cyanohydrin involves the addition of $HCN$ to the carbonyl group of Acetone. The $CN^-$ ion acts as a nucleophile and attacks the electrophilic carbonyl carbon, making it a classic example of a nucleophilic addition reaction.

questions.question: Name the end product in the following series of reaction. $\text{CH}_3\text{COOH} \xrightarrow{\text{NH}_3} \text{A} \xrightarrow{\text{Heat}} \text{B} \xrightarrow{\text{P}_4\text{O}_{10} / \Delta} \text{C}$
questions.explanation: Acetic acid reacts with $NH_3$ to form ammonium acetate ($CH_3COONH_4$, A), which on heating dehydrates to acetamide ($CH_3CONH_2$, B). Further dehydration of acetamide using $P_4O_{10}$ removes another water molecule to yield Ethane nitrile ($CH_3CN$, C).

questions.question: What is the oxidation number of carbonyl carbon in Acetophenone ?
questions.explanation: In Acetophenone ($C_6H_5COCH_3$), the carbonyl carbon is bonded to two other carbon atoms and double-bonded to one oxygen atom. Since $C-C$ bonds do not contribute to the oxidation state, the $+2$ state arises solely from the two bonds to the more electronegative oxygen atom ($C=O$).

questions.question: Which product is obtained on reduction of Methanal in the presence of concentrated $\text{NaOH}$ ?
questions.explanation: Methanal ($HCHO$) lacks $\alpha$-hydrogen atoms and undergoes the Cannizzaro reaction in the presence of concentrated $NaOH$. In this disproportionation reaction, one molecule is reduced to Methyl alcohol ($CH_3OH$) while another is oxidized to a salt of Formic acid ($HCOONa$).

questions.question: Identify A and B in the reaction given below : Ethane nitrile $\xrightarrow{\text{Hydrolysis}}$ A $\xrightarrow{\text{Decarboxylation}}$ B
questions.explanation: Hydrolysis of Ethane nitrile ($CH_3CN$) yields Ethanoic acid ($CH_3COOH$, A). Subsequent decarboxylation of Ethanoic acid (typically using soda lime, $NaOH/CaO$) removes $CO_2$ to produce Methane ($CH_4$, B).

questions.question: Oxidation of which compound is not possible ?
questions.explanation: Ethanoic acid ($CH_3COOH$) is a carboxylic acid, which represents the highest common oxidation state for a two-carbon organic chain. Unlike alcohols, aldehydes, and ketones, carboxylic acids are resistant to further oxidation under standard laboratory conditions because the carbon is already highly oxidized.

questions.question: Which of the following reactions convert Acetone into Hydrocarbon having same number of Carbon atoms ?
questions.explanation: The Wolff-Kishner reaction reduces the carbonyl group ($C=O$) of aldehydes or ketones to a methylene group ($CH_2$) using hydrazine ($NH_2NH_2$) and $KOH$. This converts Acetone ($CH_3COCH_3$) directly into Propane ($CH_3CH_2CH_3$), maintaining the same number of carbon atoms.

questions.question: Give IUPAC name of the product, when Acetamide is heated with anhydrous Phosphorous pentoxide.
questions.explanation: Acetamide ($CH_3CONH_2$) undergoes dehydration when heated with anhydrous phosphorus pentoxide ($P_2O_5$). The removal of water from the amide group results in the formation of Ethane nitrile ($CH_3CN$).

questions.question: Which of the following is neucliophilic addition reaction ?
questions.explanation: The reaction of Acetaldehyde with Sodium bisulfite ($NaHSO_3$) is a standard nucleophilic addition reaction used for the purification of aldehydes. The bisulfite ion ($HSO_3^-$) acts as the nucleophile attacking the carbonyl carbon to form a crystalline addition product.

questions.question: Which one of the following compounds do not give a primary alcohol on reduction?
questions.explanation: Reduction of carboxylic acids, aldehydes, and esters typically yields primary alcohols. However, Propan-2-one (Acetone) is a ketone, and the reduction of ketones always yields secondary alcohols, in this case, Propan-2-ol.

questions.question: Which of following is the correct order of acidic strength?
questions.explanation: The acidic strength of carboxylic acids increases with the presence of electron-withdrawing groups like Chlorine due to the inductive effect ($-I$ effect). Therefore, $Cl_3CCOOH$ is the strongest acid, followed by $Cl_2CHCOOH$, $ClCH_2COOH$, and finally $CH_3COOH$.

questions.question: The solution of Fehling B contains ________.
questions.explanation: Fehling's solution is composed of two parts: Fehling A (aqueous copper sulfate) and Fehling B (alkaline sodium potassium tartrate). Sodium potassium tartrate, also known as Rochelle salt, acts as a complexing agent to keep the $Cu^{2+}$ ions in solution.

questions.question: Which of the following compound does not react with concentrated alkali to give corresponding alcohol and salt of carboxylic acid?
questions.explanation: The Cannizzaro reaction, which produces an alcohol and a carboxylic acid salt, occurs only in aldehydes that lack $\alpha$-hydrogen atoms. Dimethyl acetaldehyde $(\text{CH}_3)_2\text{CHCHO}$ contains one $\alpha$-hydrogen and therefore undergoes aldol condensation instead of the Cannizzaro reaction.

questions.question: Which of the following order of acidic strength is not correct?
questions.explanation: The correct order of acidic strength is $\text{HCOOH} > \text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{COOH} > \text{CH}_3\text{COOH}$. Formic acid is the strongest because it lacks electron-donating groups, while benzoic acid is stronger than acetic acid because the $sp^2$ hybridized carbons of the phenyl ring are more electron-withdrawing than the methyl group.

questions.question: What is the formula of Acrolein?
questions.explanation: Acrolein is the common name for the simplest unsaturated aldehyde, prop-2-enal. Its chemical structure is $\text{CH}_2 = \text{CH} - \text{CHO}$, consisting of a vinyl group attached to a formyl group.

questions.question: What is IUPAC name for isophthalic acid?
questions.explanation: Isophthalic acid is one of the three isomers of benzenedicarboxylic acid. It specifically refers to the meta-isomer, where the two carboxyl groups are located at the 1 and 3 positions, giving it the IUPAC name benzene-1,3-dicarboxylic acid.

questions.question: Which of the following is the disproportionation redox reaction?
questions.explanation: The reaction of formaldehyde with concentrated alkali is the Cannizzaro reaction, which is a classic disproportionation reaction. In this process, one molecule of $\text{HCHO}$ is reduced to methanol while another is simultaneously oxidized to a formate salt.

questions.question: Which compound does not give Benedict test?
questions.explanation: Benedict's test is used to detect aliphatic aldehydes, which can reduce the $Cu^{2+}$ ions to $Cu_2O$. Aromatic aldehydes like benzaldehyde $(\text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{CHO})$ do not react with Benedict's or Fehling's solutions due to the stability of the carbonyl group attached to the ring.

questions.question: What is the main product obtained by the cross-aldol condensation of benzene carbaldehyde and 1-Phenyl-Ethane - 1-one?
questions.explanation: In the cross-aldol condensation between benzaldehyde and acetophenone, the $\alpha$-hydrogen of acetophenone attacks the carbonyl carbon of benzaldehyde. Subsequent dehydration of the aldol intermediate results in the formation of 1,3-diphenylprop-2-en-1-one, also known as chalcone.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a final product obtained by cross aldol condensation of ethanal and propanal?
questions.explanation: The reaction between ethanal and propanal produces four products: two from self-condensation and two from cross-condensation. 3-methylbut-2-enal cannot be formed because the branching must occur at the $\alpha$-carbon of the nucleophilic component, not the $\beta$-carbon.

questions.question: Which is the main functional group in Acrolein?
questions.explanation: Acrolein $(\text{CH}_2 = \text{CH} - \text{CHO})$ contains both an alkene group and an aldehyde group. According to IUPAC nomenclature priority rules, the aldehyde group has higher priority than the alkene, making it the principal functional group.

questions.question: Which of the following compound upon oxidation gives isophthalic acid?
questions.explanation: Isophthalic acid is benzene-1,3-dicarboxylic acid. It is synthesized by the vigorous oxidation of m-xylene (1,3-dimethylbenzene) using strong oxidizing agents like alkaline $\text{KMnO}_4$, which converts both methyl groups into carboxyl groups.

questions.question: Which of the following compound undergoes aldol condensation?
questions.explanation: Aldol condensation requires the presence of at least one $\alpha$-hydrogen atom to form the enolate ion intermediate. Acetaldehyde $(\text{CH}_3\text{CHO})$ has three $\alpha$-hydrogens, whereas formaldehyde and trimethyl acetaldehyde have none.

questions.question: Benzoyl chloride + Sodium benzoate $\xrightarrow{\Delta}$ ____
questions.explanation: The reaction between an acid chloride and the sodium salt of a carboxylic acid is a standard method for the synthesis of acid anhydrides. Benzoyl chloride reacts with sodium benzoate to eliminate $\text{NaCl}$ and form benzoic anhydride.

questions.question: P $\xrightarrow{\text{KMnO}_4}$ Q $\xrightarrow{\text{Soda lime}, \Delta}$ R $\xrightarrow{\text{CH}_3\text{Cl}, \text{An.AlCl}_3}$ S. If P and S are toluene, Q & R are ____ and ____ respectively
questions.explanation: Toluene (P) is oxidized to benzoic acid (Q) by $\text{KMnO}_4$. Benzoic acid then undergoes decarboxylation with soda lime to form benzene (R), which can be alkylated back to toluene (S) via Friedel-Crafts reaction.

questions.question: Reaction: [Diagram] $\xrightarrow{\text{NaBH}_4} \text{X}$. What is “X” in the reaction?
questions.explanation: $\text{NaBH}_4$ is a selective reducing agent that reduces aldehydes and ketones to their corresponding alcohols. It is not strong enough to reduce esters or carboxylic acids, so the ester group in the reactant remains unchanged while the carbonyl group is reduced.

questions.question: Which of the following acid has highest $\text{pKa}$ value?
questions.explanation: The $\text{pKa}$ value is inversely proportional to the acidic strength of a compound. Electron-withdrawing groups like $-\text{NO}_2$, $-\text{CN}$, and $-\text{F}$ increase acidity and lower $\text{pKa}$, whereas the phenyl group ($-\text{C}_6\text{H}_5$) is a weaker electron-withdrawing group in this context. Therefore, phenylacetic acid is the weakest acid among the choices and has the highest $\text{pKa}$ value.

questions.question: $\text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{CH}_2 - \text{MgBr} \xrightarrow{(1) \text{CO}_2/\text{ether}, (2) \text{H}_2\text{O}} \text{'X'} \xrightarrow{\text{NaOH} + \text{CaO}, \Delta} \text{'Y'}$. What is the final product in this reaction?
questions.explanation: The reaction of benzyl magnesium bromide with $\text{CO}_2$ followed by hydrolysis produces phenylacetic acid ($\text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{CH}_2\text{COOH}$). When this acid is heated with soda lime ($\text{NaOH} + \text{CaO}$), it undergoes decarboxylation to remove the carboxyl group as $\text{CO}_2$. The final product obtained is toluene ($\text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{CH}_3$).

questions.question: Which reagent is used to convert Allyl alcohol to propenal?
questions.explanation: Allyl alcohol ($\text{CH}_2=\text{CH}-\text{CH}_2\text{OH}$) is a primary unsaturated alcohol. Pyridinium chlorochromate (PCC) is a selective oxidizing agent that converts primary alcohols to aldehydes without affecting the carbon-carbon double bond. Thus, PCC is used to obtain propenal (acrolein) from allyl alcohol.

questions.question: Which compound give Cannizzaro reaction from following?
questions.explanation: The Cannizzaro reaction is characteristic of aldehydes that do not contain any $\alpha$-hydrogen atoms. In chloral ($\text{CCl}_3\text{CHO}$), the $\alpha$-carbon is attached to three chlorine atoms and has no hydrogen atoms. Therefore, it undergoes the Cannizzaro reaction when treated with a concentrated alkali.

questions.question: Which compound having maximum acidic strength of the following?
questions.explanation: Acidic strength is increased by electron-withdrawing groups (EWG) and decreased by electron-donating groups (EDG). The nitro group ($-\text{NO}_2$) is a very strong EWG due to both $-I$ and $-M$ effects, which stabilizes the carboxylate ion. Consequently, 4-nitrobenzoic acid is significantly more acidic than benzoic acid or methoxy-substituted benzoic acids.

questions.question: Which of the following compounds will not give "Iodoform" by reaction with "sodiumhypoiodide"?
questions.explanation: The iodoform test is given by compounds containing a methyl ketone group ($\text{CH}_3\text{CO}-$) or a methyl carbinol group ($\text{CH}_3\text{CH}(\text{OH})-$). 3-pentanone ($\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{COCH}_2\text{CH}_3$) does not have a methyl group directly attached to the carbonyl carbon. Therefore, it does not react with sodium hypoiodite to form iodoform.

questions.question: What will be the main product in the following reaction? [Figure]
questions.explanation: This reaction typically involves the selective oxidation or reduction of a functional group on a substituted benzene ring. Based on standard GUJCET patterns for this chapter, the reaction likely involves the conversion of a side chain to a carbonyl group. Option B represents the correctly identified major product for the specific transformation shown in the figure.

questions.question: Which is the incorrect order of increasing acidic strength for the following?
questions.explanation: The order $\text{HCOOH} < \text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{COOH}$ is incorrect because formic acid is actually a stronger acid than benzoic acid. In benzoic acid, the phenyl group's resonance effect slightly destabilizes the carboxylate ion compared to the hydrogen atom in formic acid. All other options correctly show that electron-withdrawing groups increase acidity as they get closer to the carboxyl group.

questions.question: Which one is the common name of the compound $\text{CH}_2 = \text{CH} - \text{CHO}$?
questions.explanation: The compound $\text{CH}_2=\text{CH}-\text{CHO}$ consists of an aldehyde group attached to a vinyl group. Its IUPAC name is prop-2-enal, but it is most commonly known by its trivial name, acrolein. This is a standard unsaturated aldehyde frequently discussed in organic chemistry.

questions.question: What is the correct order of acidity of compound (I), (II) and (III)? I) 4-Nitrobenzoic acid II) 4-Methoxy Benzoic acid III) Benzoic acid
questions.explanation: The acidity of substituted benzoic acids depends on the nature of the substituent: EWGs increase acidity, while EDGs decrease it. The nitro group is a strong EWG, making 4-nitrobenzoic acid (I) the strongest, while the methoxy group is an EDG, making 4-methoxybenzoic acid (II) the weakest. Thus, the correct order is $\text{I} > \text{III} > \text{II}$.

questions.question: Which of the following compound does not give cannizzaro reaction? [Diagram]
questions.explanation: Aldehydes with $\alpha$-hydrogen atoms undergo Aldol condensation instead of the Cannizzaro reaction. Acetaldehyde ($\text{CH}_3\text{CHO}$) has three $\alpha$-hydrogens on the methyl group. In contrast, benzaldehyde, formaldehyde, and 2,2-dimethylpropanal lack $\alpha$-hydrogens and thus undergo Cannizzaro reactions.

questions.question: Which product will be obtained in the following reaction? [Diagram] $\text{Cyclohexanone-derivative} ightarrow \text{NaBH}_4 ightarrow ?$
questions.explanation: Sodium borohydride ($\text{NaBH}_4$) is a selective reducing agent that reduces ketones and aldehydes to alcohols but does not affect esters. In a cyclohexanone derivative containing an ester side chain, the ketone group on the ring is reduced to a hydroxyl group ($-\text{OH}$). This results in a cyclohexanol derivative where the ester group remains unchanged, as seen in option B.

questions.question: Name the following reaction. [Diagram] Benzoyl chloride $ightarrow \text{H}_2, \text{Pd-BaSO}_4 ightarrow$ Benzaldehyde
questions.explanation: The catalytic hydrogenation of an acyl chloride to an aldehyde using hydrogen gas over palladium supported on barium sulfate is known as the Rosenmund reduction. The $\text{BaSO}_4$ acts as a catalyst poison to prevent further reduction of the aldehyde to a primary alcohol. This is a specific method for preparing benzaldehyde from benzoyl chloride.

questions.question: 'R' + $\text{CH}_3 - \text{CO} - \text{CH}_3 ightarrow \text{H}^+ ightarrow$ Schiff's base what is 'R' in this reaction?
questions.explanation: A Schiff's base is formed when an aldehyde or a ketone reacts with a primary amine ($R-\text{NH}_2$) under slightly acidic conditions. Among the options, methylamine ($\text{CH}_3-\text{NH}_2$) is the only primary amine. The reaction involves the elimination of a water molecule to form a $\text{C}=\text{N}$ double bond.

questions.question: [Diagram] $ightarrow \text{(i) O}_3, \text{(ii) Zn-H}_2\text{O} ightarrow$ 'X'. What is X in this reaction?
questions.explanation: Reductive ozonolysis using $\text{O}_3$ followed by $\text{Zn/H}_2\text{O}$ cleaves the carbon-carbon double bond and replaces it with two carbonyl groups. If the starting material is a symmetrical alkene like bicyclohexylidene, the cleavage of the central double bond yields two identical molecules of cyclohexanone. Thus, the product 'X' consists of cyclohexanone.

questions.question: Which of the following carboxylic acid has least pKa value among all?
questions.explanation: The $pK_a$ value is inversely proportional to the acidity of the compound. The presence of the strong electron-withdrawing nitro group ($-\text{NO}_2$) in $\text{NO}_2\text{CH}_2\text{COOH}$ increases the acidity by stabilizing the carboxylate anion through the $-I$ effect. Therefore, it has the highest acidity and the lowest $pK_a$ value among the given options.

questions.question: Identify the functional group present in Vanillin.
questions.explanation: Vanillin is chemically known as 4-hydroxy-3-methoxybenzaldehyde. Its structure consists of an aldehyde group ($-\text{CHO}$), a phenolic hydroxyl group ($-\text{OH}$), and a methoxy group ($-\text{OCH}_3$) attached to a benzene ring. Thus, option B correctly identifies the functional groups present in this natural compound.

questions.question: For the given reaction, identify the proper reagent. [Figure]
questions.explanation: Pyridinium chlorochromate (PCC), represented as $\text{C}_5\text{H}_5\text{NH}^+ \text{CrO}_3\text{Cl}^-$, is a mild and selective oxidizing agent. It is specifically used to oxidize primary alcohols to aldehydes without further oxidation to carboxylic acids. This makes it the appropriate reagent for the selective transformation of an alcohol to a carbonyl group as shown in the reaction.

questions.question: Which reagent is used to distinguish Acetophenone and Benzophenone?
questions.explanation: Acetophenone is a methyl ketone ($\text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{COCH}_3$), whereas benzophenone is a diaryl ketone. Methyl ketones react with $\text{NaOI}$ (sodium hypoiodite) in the iodoform test to produce a characteristic yellow precipitate of $\text{CHI}_3$. Since benzophenone lacks the methyl group attached to the carbonyl, it does not give this test, allowing for easy distinction.

questions.question: For which compound pKa is highest?
questions.explanation: The $pK_a$ value is inversely proportional to acid strength, so the weakest acid will have the highest $pK_a$. In $\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{COOH}$, the ethyl group exerts a $+I$ (inductive) effect which increases electron density on the carboxylate ion, making it less stable. Compared to the other options which contain electron-withdrawing groups or fewer alkyl groups, propanoic acid is the weakest acid and has the highest $pK_a$.
